title: Security+ SY0-701 — Section 16: Endpoint Security
description: Jason Dion Security+ SY0-701 — Section 16: Endpoint Security. Domain: Security Architecture (3.0) & Security Operations (4.0).

questions.question: What does EDR stand for and what is its purpose?
answers: Endpoint Detection and Response — detects and responds to threats on endpoints
Enterprise Data Recovery — backs up endpoint data
External Device Restriction — blocks USB devices
Encrypted Data Repository — stores encrypted files
questions.explanation: EDR (Endpoint Detection and Response) continuously monitors endpoints to detect, investigate, and respond to cybersecurity threats in real time.

questions.question: What is the difference between signature-based and heuristic antivirus detection?
answers: Signature-based matches known malware patterns; heuristic analyzes behavior for unknown threats
Signature-based is newer; heuristic is older
Signature-based is faster; heuristic is more accurate
They are the same technique
questions.explanation: Signature-based detection compares files against a database of known malware signatures. Heuristic detection uses behavioral analysis to identify potentially malicious activity from unknown threats.

questions.question: What is a host-based firewall?
answers: A software firewall installed on individual endpoints to control inbound and outbound traffic
A firewall built into network routers
A cloud-based firewall service
A hardware appliance protecting multiple servers
questions.explanation: A host-based firewall runs on individual devices (endpoints) and controls network traffic to and from that specific host, providing an additional layer of defense.

questions.question: What is Data Loss Prevention (DLP)?
answers: Technology that detects and prevents unauthorized data exfiltration
A backup system for user data
Software for recovering deleted files
A type of encryption for storage devices
questions.explanation: DLP solutions monitor, detect, and block sensitive data from leaving the organization through endpoints, network, or cloud services.

questions.question: What is Full Disk Encryption (FDE)?
answers: Encrypting all data on a storage device to protect it if the device is lost or stolen
Encrypting only sensitive files
Encrypting network traffic
Encrypting email attachments
questions.explanation: FDE encrypts the entire contents of a storage device. If the device is lost or stolen, the data remains unreadable without the encryption key. BitLocker and FileVault are common examples.

questions.question: Why is patch management critical for endpoint security?
answers: Patches fix known vulnerabilities that attackers could exploit
Patches improve system performance only
Patches are optional updates for new features
Patches reduce network bandwidth usage
questions.explanation: Patch management ensures that security vulnerabilities in operating systems and applications are remediated promptly, reducing the attack surface.

questions.question: What is application whitelisting?
answers: Allowing only approved applications to run on an endpoint
Blocking known malicious applications
Encrypting application data
Monitoring application network traffic
questions.explanation: Application whitelisting (now often called allowlisting) restricts endpoints to run only pre-approved software, preventing unauthorized or malicious applications from executing.

questions.question: What does Mobile Device Management (MDM) enable organizations to do?
answers: Remotely manage, enforce policies, and wipe mobile devices
Monitor mobile device usage statistics
Provide technical support to mobile users
Sell mobile devices to employees
questions.explanation: MDM allows IT administrators to enroll, configure, secure, and remotely manage mobile devices (phones, tablets) to enforce corporate security policies.

questions.question: What is the main security risk of BYOD (Bring Your Own Device) policies?
answers: Personal devices may lack corporate security controls, creating vulnerabilities
Company data may be too secure on personal devices
BYOD increases IT hardware costs
Personal devices are harder to support technically
questions.explanation: BYOD introduces risk because personal devices may not meet corporate security standards, may contain malware, or may lack proper encryption and patch management.

questions.question: What does a Host-based Intrusion Detection System (HIDS) do?
answers: Monitors a single host for suspicious activity and policy violations
Monitors all network traffic for intrusions
Prevents all unauthorized access attempts
Manages user authentication on a host
questions.explanation: HIDS monitors the internals of a computing system (files, logs, processes) for signs of intrusion or policy violations on that specific host.

questions.question: What is sandboxing in endpoint security?
answers: Isolating suspicious programs in a controlled environment to observe behavior
Encrypting temporary files on the endpoint
Storing data in cloud-based storage
Creating backups of critical system files
questions.explanation: Sandboxing isolates untrusted code or applications in a restricted environment where they can be analyzed without risking the host system.

questions.question: What does Secure Boot protect against?
answers: Unauthorized or malicious code running during the system boot process
Physical theft of the device
Unauthorized network access
Password brute-force attacks
questions.explanation: Secure Boot is a UEFI feature that validates the integrity of the OS bootloader and prevents unauthorized or tampered code from loading at startup.

questions.question: What is a Trusted Platform Module (TPM)?
answers: A hardware chip that provides cryptographic functions and stores security keys
A software-based password vault
A type of biometric sensor
A network authentication protocol
questions.explanation: A TPM is a dedicated microcontroller that provides hardware-based security functions, including key generation, secure storage, and platform integrity measurement.

questions.question: How does XDR (Extended Detection and Response) differ from EDR?
answers: XDR integrates telemetry across endpoints, networks, and cloud; EDR focuses only on endpoints
XDR is older than EDR
XDR only works on mobile devices
XDR requires on-premise deployment
questions.explanation: XDR extends EDR capabilities by correlating data across multiple security layers (endpoint, network, email, cloud) to provide a unified threat detection and response platform.

questions.question: Why is hardware asset management important for endpoint security?
answers: You can only secure what you know exists on your network
It reduces hardware procurement costs
It improves device performance
It simplifies user onboarding
questions.explanation: Asset management maintains an inventory of all hardware. Without knowing what devices exist on the network, organizations cannot ensure they are patched, configured, and monitored properly.
